channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Teaching Children to Handle Misinformation: Challenges and Opportunities for Schools
item.description: As schools face the challenge of teaching children how to identify and handle misinformation, they must navigate complex issues without taking a political stance.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fake Jennifer Aniston bikini body ad
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICYMI: the week's 8 biggest tech news stories from Nintendo's new alarm clock, to Toyota's revolutionary EV charging tech
item.description: ICYMI here's the biggest tech news stories from Nintendo, Toyota, Panasonic, Google Gemini and more for October 12, 2024.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Who gets to own ‘America’ online?
item.description: The handle @America is a valuable account name on any social media network—and in many cases, it’s also become a right-wing calling card. As the presidential election looms, America and its imagery have become increasingly partisan.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Ktor 3.0 switches to kotlinx.io library
item.description: Ktor 3.0, the latest version of the Kotlin-based framework for building asynchronous client- and server-side applications, is now available. Ktor 3.0 switches to the kotlinx.io library for handling data sources. The release also highlights support for server-sent events (SSE).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The IRS is rolling out its free tax-filing tool to 30 million Americans—and surprisingly, it’s great
item.description: Some 30 million Americans across the country will have a new way to file their taxes next spring: Direct File, a tool developed by the Internal Revenue Service that allows users to prepare their taxes and file online completely free of charge and without the need for any third-party provider.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Covert, Fast Company

item.title: After Hurricane Milton, a growing risk: Flooded electric cars going up in flames
item.description: Not every EV flooded by storm surge goes up in flames but it’s become frequent enough that insurers, car makers, fire chiefs and politicians have all issued warnings to EV owners.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk unveils long-awaited robotaxi
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk unveiled the electric vehicle (EV) company’s long-awaited robotaxi Thursday night, showing off a fleet of sleek self-driving cars with no steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lesser-known xargs command is a versatile time saver
item.description: The xargs command may be one that many Linux users don’t know, but it’s also one that can save a lot of time and trouble on the command line. It’s an unusual command with some very handy uses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is digital transformation? Ongoing reinvention
item.description: Digital transformation definition Digital transformation is an organization-wide strategy aimed at leveraging digital technologies to modernize key business processes and to introduce new services that better engage customers, support employees, improve operations, and drive business value to the bo
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting the timing right at Setterwalls to invest in AI support
item.description: In recent years, there’ve been a number of smaller AI products geared toward the legal profession, but it wasn’t until gen AI caught on that Swedish law firm Setterwalls really saw the benefit. “We saw that there was potential to benefit on a broad front,” says CIO Daniel Sandberg.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI sees continued attempts to use AI models for election interference
item.description: OpenAI has seen continued attempts by cybercriminals to use its artificial intelligence (AI) models for fake content aimed at interfering with this year’s elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s Drasi: A lightweight approach to event-driven programming
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Incubations team is one of the more interesting components of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud. It is something between a traditional software development team and a research organization working to build solutions to the problems of distributed systems at massive scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Atlassian’s genAI assistant Rovo is now available
item.description: Atlassian’s Rovo generative AI (genAI) assistant, which launched as a preview release in May, is now available for users. The company bills Rovo as an “AI-powered” knowledge discovery tool that lets teams access information across their organization more efficiently.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: WebSockets under the hood with Node.js
item.description: WebSockets is a network communication protocol that enables two-way client-server communication. WebSockets are often used for applications that require instantaneous updates, using a persistent duplex channel atop HTTP to support real-time interactions without constant connection negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service
item.description: The new update allows users to connect their phones to satellite, as long its an iPhone 14 or later model running iOS18.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The high-speed clay technique shaking up home construction
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how Clay’s impact printing technology enables fast, sustainable construction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation
